stories.name: backend-use-state-machine-for-fatal-errors
stories.title: Replace MessageType.ERROR with _emit_state_changed for all fatal backend errors
stories.description: Replace the legacy MessageType.ERROR emission in _init_backend with _emit_state_changed(server, 'error', message=str(e), fatal=True). Also replace the worker error emission in _start_image_delivery (which is also fatal/state-changing). Update the existing test test_init_backend_failure_sends_fatal_error to assert the new STATE_CHANGED behavior.
stories.notes: FR2 (emit loading state) is already implemented from epic-emit-loading-state-on-backend-init. Only FR1 and the FR3 audit fix are needed here.

stories.name: frontend-add-error-message-handler
stories.title: Add case 'error': branch to frontend handleMessage switch
stories.description: Add an explicit case 'error': handler in the handleMessage switch in src-tauri/ui/main.ts. For fatal=true payloads, delegate to handleFatalError(payload.message). For non-fatal payloads, display a brief error notification in the loading prompt area (3 seconds). This ensures legacy non-fatal MessageType.ERROR events from operational handlers are not silently dropped.

includes: New ErrorPayload type interface if needed

stories.notes: The frontend already handles state='error' in handleStateChanged. This only adds the legacy 'error' message type handler for defense-in-depth.

dependency_notes: Stories are independent. Story 01 changes backend, Story 02 changes frontend. No mocks required.
